audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in charray.c are safe: functions with nullable parameters have explicit in-function guards (`ldap_charray_free` L116, `ldap_charray_inlist` L137), `LDAP_STRDUP` delegates to `ber_strdup_x` which handles NULL input gracefully (memory.c:637-639), and the remaining functions (`ldap_charray_add`, `ldap_charray_merge`, `ldap_charray_dup`, `ldap_charray2str`) rely on non-null caller contracts that all call sites in the codebase honour — callers pass address-of-local-variable (cannot be NULL), guard with explicit null checks before calling, or verify upstream allocations succeeded before proceeding.
